title: IBS Mean Reversion on SPY - Comprehensive Research
thesis:
The Internal Bar Strength (IBS) indicator measures where the close sits within the day's range: IBS = (Close - Low) / (High - Low). Values near 0 indicate the close is near the low (intraday selling pressure/weakness), and values near 1 indicate the close is near the high (buying pressure/strength). 

The mean reversion thesis: When SPY has an extreme closing IBS (IBS < 0.20), it signals short-term selling exhaustion/capitulation. Over the next 5 trading days, the market tends to mean revert upwards as the initial selling pressure dissipates. This is especially effective when combined with a longer-term uptrend filter (Close > SMA200).

The vault already implements several IBS variants: plain ibs_spy (IBS<0.20, 5-day hold), ibs_trend (IBS<0.20 + SMA200 + ToM), ibs_dynamic (dynamic exits with ATR stop/profit take), and volume_scaled_ibs (volume-adjusted thresholds with 0.15/0.20/0.25).
signal_definition:
Entry: IBS = (Close - Low) / (High - Low) < threshold (typically 0.20), possibly with Close > SMA200 trend filter.
Exit: 5-day time-based hold, or dynamic exits (profit take at prior day's high, trailing ATR stop, or max hold).
Sizing: 95% of equity per trade (percent_of_equity) or fixed-risk 10% of capital (volume_scaled_ibs).
Execution: next_open (default) to avoid lookahead bias.

run_cards.notes: ibs_spy baseline: IBS<0.20, 5-day hold, next_open, 95% equity. 10-year backtest 2016-2025. CAGR 8.76%, Sharpe 0.70, max DD 21%. 230 trades, 64.3% win rate.
ibs_trend: IBS<0.20 + Close>SMA200 + TurnOfMonth. Only 24 trades in 10 years (very sparse). High win rate 70.8% and PF 2.92, but CAGR only 1.79% due to low signal frequency.

ibs_dynamic: IBS<0.20 + SMA200, dynamic exits (profit take at prior high, 2x ATR trailing stop, 5-day max hold). Lower CAGR 3.44% than plain ibs_spy because dynamic exits cut winners short. Better DD (11.6% vs 21%).
ibs_spy baseline re-run confirmed: IBS<0.20, 5-day hold, next_open, 95% equity. CAGR 8.76%, Sharpe 0.70, max DD 21.0%. 230 trades, 64.3% win rate. Best Sharpe among all IBS variants tested.

ibs_dynamic re-run confirmed: IBS<0.20 + SMA200, dynamic exits (profit take at prior high, 2x ATR trailing stop, 5-day max hold). CAGR 3.44%, Sharpe 0.53. Dynamic exits reduce avg hold to 2.84 days but cut winners short — expectancy drops from $643 to $244.

ibs_trend re-run confirmed: IBS<0.20 + SMA200 + TurnOfMonth. Only 24 trades — very sparse. Win rate 70.8% and PF 2.92 are best quality but CAGR only 1.79% due to low frequency. Best for risk-averse, low-frequency style.
